welcomeMessage.en: It was great having Kiki, Yating, and Peter practicing together today.
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天很高兴有Kiki、Yating和Peter一起练习。

welcomeMessage.en: Today, we talked about our Daily Routines, morning habits, and how we organize our day.
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天，我们谈论了日常作息、早晨习惯以及我们如何安排一天的时间。

welcomeMessage.en: Keep practicing! You are all doing wonderful.
welcomeMessage.zh: 继续练习！你们都做得很棒。

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Are you an early bird or a night owl? Why?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你是早起的人还是夜猫子？为什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is the first thing you do when you open your eyes in the morning?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 早上睁开眼睛后你做的第一件事是什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What time do you usually wake up on a normal work day versus the weekend?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 在正常工作日和周末，你通常什么时候起床？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What do you usually eat for breakfast?
If you were a strict life manager, what daily routine would you create for a very lazy person?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 如果你是一个严格的生活管理者，你会为一个非常懒惰的人制定什么样的日常作息？

vocabulary.definition: The usual series of things that you do at a particular time.
vocabulary.translation: 日常作息 / 日常流程
vocabulary.example: Having a healthy daily routine makes you more productive at work.

vocabulary.definition: A person who gets up or arrives early.
vocabulary.example: Kiki wakes up at 6:40 AM; she is definitely an early bird.

vocabulary.definition: A person who naturally stays up until late at night.
vocabulary.example: If you go to sleep at 12:00 AM or later, you are a night owl.

vocabulary.definition: To wake up and leave your bed.
vocabulary.example: It is so hard to get out of bed on cold winter mornings.

vocabulary.definition: To clean one's face with water.
vocabulary.example: The first thing I do is wash my face to help me wake up.

vocabulary.definition: A message that is automatically sent to you to tell you there is something new to look at.
vocabulary.translation: 通知 (手机或智能手表上的)
vocabulary.example: I check my smartwatch for new messages and notifications.

vocabulary.definition: A type of bread made with yeast and flour, cooked by steaming.
vocabulary.example: For breakfast, I usually eat a steamed bun and an egg.

vocabulary.definition: A breakfast food made from roasted grain, typically eaten with milk.
vocabulary.example: Eating cereal with cold milk is a very fast and easy breakfast.

vocabulary.definition: Demanding that rules concerning behavior are obeyed and observed.
vocabulary.example: My new life manager is very strict about my sleep schedule.

sessionSummary: Welcome to Peter for joining the class today! It was great having all three of you (Kiki, Yating, and Peter) practicing together.
We started with a big review of our last lesson, going over hobbies, free time, and chores like doing the laundry.
Then, we moved on to today's main topic: Daily Routines! We discussed normal wake-up times during the week versus how late we sleep in on the weekends.
We learned fun idioms like 'Early Bird' and 'Night Owl', and talked about our morning habits—like checking notifications on our phones or smartwatches.
Finally, we practiced organizing a healthy schedule through a fun 'Life Manager' roleplay!

keyVocabulary.translation: 日常作息 / 日常流程
keyVocabulary.example: "Having a healthy daily routine makes you more productive at work."
"Kiki wakes up at 6:40 AM; she is definitely an early bird."
"If you go to sleep at 12:00 AM or later, you are a night owl."
"It is so hard to get out of bed on cold winter mornings."
"The first thing I do is wash my face to help me wake up."
"I check my smartwatch for new messages and notifications."

grammarTips.title: Talking About Your Phone
grammarTips.description: In English, we do not say 'play phone'. The phone is the tool, not the game.
grammarTips.incorrect: "I wake up and play my phone."
grammarTips.correct: "I wake up and look at my phone." or "I play games on my phone."

grammarTips.title: Present Tense vs. Past Tense for Time
grammarTips.description: Use the Present Tense for your daily habits. Use the Past Tense for what happened today.
grammarTips.correct: "Today I woke up at 7:30 AM. (But I usually wake up at 7:00 AM)."

grammarTips.title: Pronunciation: Notification vs. Vacation
grammarTips.description: These two words sound a bit similar but mean very different things! Make sure to clearly pronounce the 'N' sound in Notification.
grammarTips.incorrect: "I got a vacation on my phone."
grammarTips.correct: "I got a notification on my phone."

grammarTips.title: Useful Native Phrase: First thing in the morning
grammarTips.description: Use this phrase to talk about the very first action of your day. It sounds much more natural than saying 'The number one thing I do'.
grammarTips.incorrect: "The first do in morning I wash my face."
grammarTips.correct: "First thing in the morning, I wash my face."

goals: Encourage students to use specific times when describing their daily routines (e.g., 'at 7:00 AM').
Help them distinguish between habits (Present Simple) and specific past events (Past Simple).
Reinforce the correct pronunciation of new vocabulary like 'notification' and phase out direct translations like 'play phone'.

phases.title: Phase 1: Warm-Up & Review (15-20 Minutes)
phases.description: Review the previous lesson's topics (hobbies, chores). Welcome Peter to the class.
questions: Did anyone do any chores like doing the laundry this week?
Did you have any spare time to practice your hobbies?

phases.title: Phase 2: Introducing Daily Routines (20-30 Minutes)
phases.description: Dive into morning and evening routines. Introduce idioms like Early Bird and Night Owl.
questions: Are you an early bird or a night owl? What time do you naturally wake up?
What is the first thing you do in the morning? (Prompt: check notifications, wash face)
What do you typically have for breakfast? (e.g., steamed bun, cereal)

phases.title: Phase 3: The 'Life Manager' Roleplay (10-15 Minutes)
phases.description: Put students in pairs. One is the 'Strict Life Manager' and the other is a 'Lazy Person'. The manager must create a new, strict daily routine for them.
questions: Manager: What time should they wake up? What must they do first thing in the morning?
Lazy Person: Try to negotiate or complain about the new routine!
